_doc: Each entry is a check. 'proves' says what passing means. 'symptom' says what failing means.

locks._: ═══ STATIC — grep source code, no infra, 0s ═══

locks.proves: login redirects to / (then /meetings), not to disabled /agent page
locks.symptom: After login, user redirected to /agent (disabled feature) instead of /meetings

locks.proves: /api/auth/me uses only cookie for identity, never falls back to env var
locks.symptom: /api/auth/me returns wrong user — fell back to VEXA_API_KEY env var instead of cookie

locks.proves: cookie Secure flag based on actual protocol, not NODE_ENV (send-magic-link)
locks.symptom: Login loop on HTTP — Secure cookie flag set based on NODE_ENV instead of actual protocol

locks.proves: cookie Secure flag based on actual protocol, not NODE_ENV (verify)
locks.symptom: Login loop on HTTP — Secure cookie flag set based on NODE_ENV instead of actual protocol

locks.proves: cookie Secure flag based on actual protocol, not NODE_ENV (admin-verify)
locks.symptom: Login loop on HTTP — Secure cookie flag set based on NODE_ENV instead of actual protocol

locks.proves: cookie Secure flag based on actual protocol, not NODE_ENV (nextauth)
locks.symptom: Login loop on HTTP — Secure cookie flag set based on NODE_ENV instead of actual protocol

locks.proves: bot detail route is /bots/id/{id}, not /bots/{id} which collides with /bots/status
locks.symptom: GET /bots/status returns 422 — Starlette matches /bots/{meeting_id} before /bots/status

locks.proves: self_initiated_leave during stopping treated as completed, not failed
locks.symptom: Bot shows failed after successful meeting — exit during stopping treated as failure

locks.proves: MeetingCreate schema has parse_meeting_url — accepts meeting_url field directly
locks.symptom: All Teams URL formats return 422 — MeetingCreate schema had no URL parser

locks.proves: dashboard API client maps native_meeting_id to platform_specific_id via mapMeeting
locks.symptom: Transcript page empty — getMeeting() skipped mapMeeting(), native_meeting_id not mapped

locks.proves: dashboard and admin-api share same ADMIN_API_TOKEN default in compose
locks.symptom: Dashboard login 503 — VEXA_ADMIN_API_KEY had different default than admin-api

locks.proves: Chrome uses basic password store — cookies decryptable across sessions
locks.symptom: Authenticated join fails — Chrome cookies encrypted, login not restored across sessions

locks.proves: dashboard VEXA_ADMIN_API_KEY equals admin-api ADMIN_API_TOKEN — login will work
locks.symptom: Dashboard login fails silently — VEXA_ADMIN_API_KEY in dashboard differs from admin-api ADMIN_API_TOKEN

locks.proves: dashboard can authenticate to admin-api — user lookup and login will work
locks.symptom: Dashboard shows empty pages — VEXA_ADMIN_API_KEY rejected by admin-api

locks.proves: dashboard VEXA_API_KEY accepted by gateway — transcript and bot data will load
locks.symptom: Dashboard transcript/status empty — VEXA_API_KEY in dashboard is stale (401 from gateway)

locks.proves: dashboard knows where the backend is — API calls will reach the gateway
locks.symptom: Dashboard cannot reach backend — VEXA_API_URL not set or wrong

locks.proves: meeting-api can reach MinIO — browser state save/restore and recordings will work
locks.symptom: Authenticated bot join has no S3 config — MINIO_ENDPOINT not set in meeting-api

locks.proves: MinIO bucket configured — file operations have a destination
locks.symptom: Browser session save fails — MINIO_BUCKET not configured in meeting-api

locks.proves: meeting-api can reach runtime-api — bot container creation will work
locks.symptom: Bot creation fails — meeting-api cannot reach runtime-api

locks.proves: API gateway accepts connections — all client requests can reach backend
locks.symptom: API gateway not responding

locks.proves: admin-api responds with valid token — user management and login work
locks.symptom: Admin API not responding

locks.proves: dashboard serves pages — user can access the UI
locks.symptom: Dashboard not responding

locks.proves: dashboard /api/config returns a browser-resolvable wsUrl — WebSocket will connect
locks.symptom: Dashboard WS broken — wsUrl contains internal Docker hostname (api-gateway, etc.) instead of localhost or public host

locks.proves: runtime-api responds — bot container lifecycle management works
locks.symptom: Runtime API not responding — bot creation will fail

locks.proves: transcription service responds — audio can be converted to text
locks.symptom: Transcription service not responding

locks.proves: Redis responds to PING — WebSocket pub/sub, session state, and caching work
locks.symptom: Redis not responding — WS, session state, and pub/sub will fail

locks.proves: MinIO responds — recordings and browser state storage work
locks.symptom: MinIO not responding — recordings and browser state will fail

locks.proves: all vexa deployments have ready replicas — K8s rollout succeeded
locks.symptom: One or more vexa deployments have 0 ready replicas

locks.proves: no pods in CrashLoopBackOff — all containers start cleanly
locks.symptom: Pod stuck in CrashLoopBackOff — check image, env vars, resource limits

locks.proves: pods are stable — restart count below threshold (5)
locks.symptom: Pod restarting frequently — OOMKill, liveness probe failure, or crash

locks.proves: K8s secrets created — services can read credentials from secret mounts
locks.symptom: No vexa secrets found — helm install may have skipped secret creation

locks.proves: helm chart passes lint — templates are well-formed
locks.symptom: helm lint fails — chart has structural errors

locks.proves: helm template renders without errors — values and templates are consistent
locks.symptom: helm template fails — missing required values or template syntax error

locks.proves: database schema has all required columns — schema sync ran successfully
locks.symptom: Services crash or return 500 — missing columns, tables, or indexes after DB migration

locks.proves: users table has data — not an empty database
locks.symptom: No users in database — dump not loaded or load failed silently

locks.proves: API tokens have scopes assigned — backfill migration ran
locks.symptom: Tokens have empty scopes — scope enforcement will reject all requests

locks.proves: browser session CDP proxy is reachable — gateway can connect to browser container
locks.symptom: Failed to reach browser container: Name or service not known — pod IP not resolved in K8s

locks.proves: POST /bots with mode=browser_session returns 201 — browser-session profile exists and works
locks.symptom: Failed to start browser session container — browser-session profile missing from runtime-api

locks.proves: bots are created with recordingEnabled=true — audio will be captured and uploaded
locks.symptom: No audio recording for meeting — RECORDING_ENABLED env var not set or recordingEnabled=false in BOT_CONFIG

locks.proves: MinIO bucket exists and is writable — recordings and browser state can be stored
locks.symptom: No audio recording for meeting — MinIO bucket not created or not writable

locks.proves: segments injected into Redis appear in the REST transcript — collector pipeline works end-to-end
locks.symptom: Transcript empty despite audio being captured — collector not processing Redis stream, or segments not reaching Postgres

locks.proves: bot status transitions from requested within 30s — callbacks from bot→meeting-api work
locks.symptom: Bot stuck in 'requested' — status change callbacks not reaching meeting-api (wrong image, missing callback URL, network issue)

locks.proves: POST /bots creates a bot without server error — runtime-api orchestrator is functional
locks.symptom: Bot creation returns 500 — runtime-api cannot spawn bot containers (wrong orchestrator, missing image, or broken config)

locks.proves: GET /bots/status returns 200 — no route collision with /bots/{meeting_id}
locks.symptom: GET /bots/status returns 422 due to route collision with /bots/{meeting_id}

locks.proves: GET /meetings returns 200 — meeting list API works
locks.symptom: GET /meetings not returning 200

locks.proves: API rejects unauthenticated requests — auth middleware is active
locks.symptom: API accepts requests without auth token

locks.proves: Teams standard join URL accepted by POST /bots
locks.symptom: Teams standard URL format rejected

locks.proves: Teams /meet/ shortlink URL parsed and accepted by POST /bots (no explicit platform needed)
locks.symptom: Teams shortlink URL format rejected — parse_meeting_url does not handle /meet/ path

locks.proves: Teams channel meeting URL accepted or known gap
locks.symptom: Teams channel meeting URL rejected

locks.proves: Teams enterprise domain URL parsed and accepted by POST /bots (no explicit platform needed)
locks.symptom: Teams custom enterprise domain URL rejected

locks.proves: Teams personal (teams.live.com) URL parsed and accepted by POST /bots (no explicit platform needed)
locks.symptom: Teams personal (teams.live.com) URL rejected

locks.proves: Google Meet URL accepted by POST /bots — parser handles GMeet format
locks.symptom: Google Meet URL not accepted by POST /bots

locks.proves: garbage URLs rejected with 400/422 — input validation works
locks.symptom: Invalid URL accepted instead of rejected

locks.proves: WebSocket connection works — dashboard live updates will function
locks.symptom: WebSocket not responding to ping

locks.proves: magic link login endpoint returns 200 — admin key is correctly configured
locks.symptom: Dashboard magic link login returns 503 — admin key misconfigured

locks.proves: transcription API token accepted — bots can convert audio to text
locks.symptom: Transcription returns 403 — TRANSCRIPTION_SERVICE_TOKEN expired or invalid. Bots capture audio but produce 0 segments.

locks.proves: dashboard HTML has cache control — deploy won't serve stale JS bundles
locks.symptom: Old JS bundle served after deploy — HTML page cached by browser

locks.proves: Teams handleTeamsAudioData has VAD gating (not bypassed)
locks.symptom: Teams audio reaches Whisper without VAD filtering — noise/hallucinations

locks.proves: Teams emitEvent drives activeDomSpeakers for audio routing
locks.symptom: Teams audio routing falls back to caption-only — fragile activation
